title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: 1. High: [freee-client.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L168) retries every failure path for every tool, including `freee_create_deal` (`POST /api/1/deals`). If freee creates the deal but the response is truncated, unparsable, or times out, attempts 2-3 will submit duplicate expense entries. Retries here need to be limited to idempotent calls or guarded with dedup/idempotency. 2. Medium: [freee-client.mjs](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L181) swallows non-JSON/shape errors from `r.result.content[0].text` as if they were harmless line-parse failures. That converts real upstream failures into `no result` + retry, which both hides the root cause and amplifies the duplicate-write risk above. 3. Low: [eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L347) removed the old CJK-text skip in bash fences. Japanese prose lines inside ```bash blocks now get interpreted as candidate commands and penalized as phantom binaries, so bilingual `SKILL.md` files will score incorrectly. Assumptions: reviewed the tracked working-tree diff only; most `experience.jsonl` churn looks generated and I did not treat that as product code. No separate direct secret-exposure issue stood out beyond the retry/data-integrity problem.
